title: PSI to Bar Converter
description: Industrial-grade pressure unit converter with validation, loss analysis, and confidence-adjusted output. Compliant with ISO 80000-1, Lean Six Sigma, and WERC standards.

inputs.label: Pressure (PSI)
inputs.businessContext: Input pressure in pounds per square inch (PSI). Typical range: 0–100,000 psi for industrial applications.

businessContext_i18n.en: Input pressure in pounds per square inch (PSI). Typical range: 0–100,000 psi for industrial applications.

inputs.label: Temperature (°C)
inputs.businessContext: Process temperature in Celsius. Used for thermal correction per ISO 80000-1.

businessContext_i18n.en: Process temperature in Celsius. Used for thermal correction per ISO 80000-1.

inputs.label: Altitude (m)
inputs.businessContext: Altitude above sea level for barometric correction. Relevant for high-altitude installations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Altitude above sea level for barometric correction. Relevant for high-altitude installations.

inputs.label: Fluid Type
inputs.businessContext: Select fluid type for density correction. Options: water, oil, gas, steam.

businessContext_i18n.en: Select fluid type for density correction. Options: water, oil, gas, steam.

inputs.label: Apply Temperature Correction
inputs.businessContext: Enable thermal expansion correction for pressure measurement.

businessContext_i18n.en: Enable thermal expansion correction for pressure measurement.

inputs.label: Apply Altitude Correction
inputs.businessContext: Enable barometric pressure correction based on altitude.

businessContext_i18n.en: Enable barometric pressure correction based on altitude.

rules.message: Pressure must be between 0 and 100,000 psi.
Altitude must be between -500 m and 10,000 m.
Temperature correction is recommended for extreme temperatures outside -10°C to 50°C.

thresholds.message: Pressure exceeds 10,000 psi. Verify equipment rating per ISO 4126.
Pressure exceeds 50,000 psi. Immediate safety check required.
Temperature below -20°C. Risk of fluid freezing or material brittleness.
Altitude above 3000 m. Barometric correction strongly advised.

formulas.name: Temperature Correction Factor
formulas.description: Linear thermal expansion correction for pressure measurement (ISO 80000-1). Reference temperature 20°C.

formulas.name: Altitude Correction Factor
formulas.description: Barometric pressure correction based on altitude using barometric formula (simplified).

formulas.name: Fluid Density Factor
formulas.description: Correction factor based on fluid type relative to water at 20°C.
formulas.formula: density_factor = CASE fluid_type WHEN 'water' THEN 1.0 WHEN 'oil' THEN 0.85 WHEN 'gas' THEN 0.0012 WHEN 'steam' THEN 0.0006 ELSE 1.0 END

formulas.name: Corrected Pressure in Bar
formulas.description: Applies selected corrections to base conversion.

formulas.name: Data Confidence Score
formulas.description: Confidence score based on number of corrections applied and input validity (0 to 1).

formulas.name: Primary Result (Adjusted Bar)
formulas.description: Final pressure in bar adjusted by confidence score for reporting.

primary.label: Adjusted Pressure (bar)
primary.description: Final pressure value after all corrections and confidence adjustment.

temp_factor.label: Temperature Correction Factor
temp_factor.description: Factor applied if temperature correction enabled.

alt_factor.label: Altitude Correction Factor
alt_factor.description: Factor applied if altitude correction enabled.

density_factor.label: Fluid Density Factor
density_factor.description: Factor based on selected fluid type.

confidence.label: Data Confidence Score
confidence.description: Confidence level (0 to 1) based on corrections and input quality.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Uncorrected Temperature Drift
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If temperature correction is disabled, measurement error can be up to 0.5% per 10°C deviation from 20°C.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Uncorrected Altitude Error
hiddenLossDrivers.description: At 3000 m altitude, barometric pressure is ~30% lower, causing systematic overestimation if uncorrected.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Fluid Density Mismatch
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Using water density for oil or gas can introduce errors of 15% or more.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Sensor Calibration Drift
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Over time, pressure sensors may drift. Recommended recalibration per ISO 10012 every 6 months.

suggestedActions.label: Enable Temperature Correction
suggestedActions.description: For processes with temperature variation > ±10°C, enable temperature correction to maintain accuracy.

suggestedActions.label: Enable Altitude Correction
suggestedActions.description: For installations above 1000 m, enable altitude correction to avoid systematic error.

suggestedActions.label: Verify Fluid Type
suggestedActions.description: Ensure the correct fluid type is selected. For mixed fluids, consult material safety data sheet.

suggestedActions.label: Schedule Sensor Calibration
suggestedActions.description: Plan recalibration of pressure sensor per ISO 10012 to maintain measurement traceability.

suggestedActions.label: Document Measurement Uncertainty
suggestedActions.description: Record all correction factors and confidence score for audit trail and quality management (Lean Six Sigma).

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Pressure
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Primary result multiplied by confidence score for conservative reporting.
